title: Friday Sermon 13 September 2002
description:
O ye who believe!  Be mindful of Allah and have firm faith in His Messenger for He will give you a double portion of His mercy and will provide for you a light wherein you will walk and will grant you forgiveness – For verily Allah is Most Forgiving, Ever Merciful.

('The Holy Quran'.  Al Hadid [Iron]: 29).

If the believers abide by and discharge of their obligations they will be granted singular blessings and beneficence, the verse terms these blessings as Noor or light.

The Promised Messiah (on whom be peace) elucidated that the light granted to chosen people is manifested in the form of revelation and visions and it descends upon the hearts of these people.*

With reference to the Day of Judgement he said that this light or spiritual light that has been granted in an unseen and concealed manner to these special people of God, would surround them and would be exhibited openly for all to behold:

 … On that day you will see that the light of the believers which remain hidden in the world will manifest itself by, running before them and on their right hands; …

(Ahmad.  'Islami Usul Ki Philosophy' ['The Philosophy of the Teachings of Islam'].  Matba Siddiqi, 1896, published 1905.  'Rūhāni Khazāin: x'. 411.  English: Islam International Publications, Ltd, 2010.  153, 154).

The Promised Messiah (on whom be peace) enjoined that if the believers adopt righteousness Allah the Exalted distinguishes them from the rest and this distinction is evident in the shape of the light of revelation, wonders and miracles that they are associated with.

(Ahmad.  'Aina e kamalat e Islam'.  Riyaz e Hind, 1893.  'Ruhani Khazain: v'. 296).

Some revelations and some Persian verses by the Promised Messiah (on whom be peace) also depict the splendorous light bestowed on the Holy Prophet (peace and blessings be on him).

* Hazrat Mirza Ghulam Ahmad.  'Hamamat al-Bushra'.  Punjab Press, 1894.  'Ruhani Khazain: xvii'.  298.

The text https://www.alislam.org/urdu/sermon/FST20020913-UR.pdf
